# v1.3.0 — Discrimination Frontier + Vendor Surface

**Released:** 2026-XX-XX (date set at tag time)
**Sibling parity bar:** cre-agent-audit v0.2.x discrimination-frontier + vendor-surface cycle
**Tag:** [v1.3.0](https://github.com/linus10x/finserv-agent-audit/releases/tag/v1.3.0)
**Zenodo DOI:** [pending re-issue at tag time]

---

## What changed since v1.2.0

v1.2 shipped the OCC 2026-13 white-space response, two new regulatory mappings (Treasury FS AI RMF + NIST AI 600-1 GenAI Profile), the first wave of ecosystem onramps (MCP server adapter, OpenTelemetry GenAI emitter, GitHub Actions composite action + reusable workflow, CLI tool), the four buyer-conversation closers, the maturity-model + CLI self-score, the drift-detection test pack, the `ProtectedClassProxyDetector` real implementation (mutual-information arm), and the PyPI Trusted Publishing release pipeline.

v1.3 ships the **discrimination frontier + vendor surface** — the two surfaces that v1.2's enterprise-buyer conversations surfaced as the next bar. Seven new governance modules across two thematic clusters: a discrimination-frontier cluster (LDA search beyond mutual-information, LLM-as-classifier disparate-impact harness, effective-challenge harness, customer-facing chatbot guardrail) and a vendor-surface cluster (vendor-attestation ledger, retraining-cadence monitor, deprecation-watch). Seven new ADRs (0020-0026), six new regulatory + incident + disclosure docs, and the sixth FSI vendor class (foundation-model API providers).

Maintainer note: this notes file anticipates the full shape of v1.3 across Tranches A-D. Refresh the bullets below from `CHANGELOG.md` `[1.3.0]` once Tranche A-D commits land — only the surfaces that actually shipped should remain in the highlight list at tag time.

## Highlights

### Code — Discrimination-frontier cluster (Tranche A)

- **`LDASearchHarness`** (`lda_search.py`, ADR-0020) — Equally-accurate-less-discriminatory alternative search. Iterates a candidate set of feature-removal, threshold-shift, and monotonic-constraint variants and surfaces the EALD frontier (model variants that meet a target disparate-impact ratio at the lowest accuracy cost). Anchored to ECOA effects-test doctrine and CFPB Circular 2023-09. Complements the v1.2 `ProtectedClassProxyDetector` mutual-information arm.
- **`LLMDisparateImpactHarness`** (`llm_disparate_impact_harness.py`, ADR-0021) — EEOC 4/5ths-rule disparate-impact testing applied to LLM-agent outputs (hiring screens, lending narratives, fraud-score rationales). Anchored to *Mobley v. Workday* (N.D. Cal., third-party-LLM-as-employer's-agent theory). Pluggable LLM-classifier interface so adopters can swap in their production model.
- **`EffectiveChallengeHarness`** (`effective_challenge_harness.py`, ADR-0022) — Frontier-API model-validation surface satisfying SR 11-7's effective-challenge requirement + OCC 2026-13's third-party-model expectations. Pluggable challenger-model interface (use a peer foundation model from a different vendor as the challenge witness).

### Code — Vendor-surface cluster (Tranche B)

- **`VendorAttestationLedger`** (`vendor_attestation_ledger.py`, ADR-0023) — Append-only chain-of-custody ledger for third-party model attestations: model cards, evaluation reports, incident notices, training-data exclusion attestations. Per Treasury FS AI RMF + DORA Art. 28 third-party-risk requirements. Persists through the existing `LedgerStore` Protocol seam.
- **`RetrainingCadenceMonitor`** (`retraining_cadence_monitor.py`, ADR-0024) — Weekly / monthly / continuous fine-tune validation cadence assertion. Detects when a vendor's actual cadence slips beyond the declared model-card cadence and emits a drift event. Per SR 11-7 ongoing-monitoring + OCC 2026-13 third-party-model expectations.
- **`DeprecationWatch`** (`deprecation_watch.py`, ADR-0025) — Vendor model deprecation calendar with sunset-date assertions. Emits the new `AuditEventType.DEPRECATION_ALERT` enum value when a model's sunset enters the configured warning horizon. Backstops the "vendor sunset us at 60 days notice" failure mode that hit multiple FSI tenants in 2025.

### Code — Customer-facing chatbot guardrail (Tranche C)

- **`CustomerFacingChatbotGuardrail`** (`customer_facing_chatbot_guardrail.py`, ADR-0026) — Policy-grounded RAG + commitment-interception + fabricated-policy block. Three-tier defense: (1) RAG ground every customer response in approved policy text; (2) intercept any response that creates a unilateral firm commitment before it ships; (3) block any response that asserts a policy text not present in the grounded corpus. Anchored to *Moffatt v. Air Canada* (B.C. CRT, 2024 — firm bound by chatbot's fabricated bereavement-fare policy) and EU AI Act Art. 13 transparency requirements.

### Schema

- `AuditEventType` enum gains `DEPRECATION_ALERT = "vendor.deprecation_alert"` for the `DeprecationWatch` surface.

### Documentation — Seven new ADRs

0020 LDA Search · 0021 LLM Disparate-Impact Harness · 0022 Effective Challenge Harness · 0023 Vendor Attestation Ledger · 0024 Retraining Cadence Monitor · 0025 Deprecation Watch · 0026 Customer-Facing Chatbot Guardrail.

### Documentation — Six new regulatory + incident + disclosure docs (Tranche D)

- **`docs/nydfs_part_500_ai_mapping.md`** — New York DFS 23 NYCRR 500 AI overlay. Maps the cybersecurity-program controls (governance, third-party risk, incident response, MFA, encryption) to the v1.1-v1.3 governance surface.
- **`docs/cfpb_ai_lending_supervisory_landscape.md`** — Consolidated CFPB supervisory landscape on AI in consumer lending: adverse-action, marketing, model risk, third-party oversight. Anchors the v1.2 `ProtectedClassProxyDetector` and v1.3 `LDASearchHarness` to the regulator's stated posture.
- **`docs/cfpb_circular_2023_09_mapping.md`** — CFPB Circular 2023-09 (AVMs / algorithmic appraisal) overlay. Connects the algorithmic-appraisal supervisory posture to `EquityAudit`, `LDASearchHarness`, and `VendorAttestationLedger`.
- **`docs/state_ag_enforcement_matrix.md`** — Multi-jurisdiction state attorney-general AI-enforcement actions catalog. Tracks the AG-level actions that move ahead of federal enforcement.
- **`docs/ai_incident_retrospective_template.md`** — Post-incident retrospective template aligned to NIST AI RMF GOVERN-6.2. Drop-in template for the "what happened, what we did, what we'd change" write-up regulators and audit teams ask for after a public AI incident.
- **`docs/disclosure_artifact_templates.md`** — Drop-in adverse-action / model-use / vendor-AI disclosure templates. Pre-cleared language that satisfies FCRA § 615, ECOA Reg-B § 1002.9, CFPB Circular 2022-03, and the emerging state-level model-use disclosure floor.

### Documentation — Foundation-model vendor-clauses (sixth vendor class)

- **`vendor-clauses/foundation_model_api_vendor_clauses.md`** — Sales-tool-grade addendum for foundation-model API providers (OpenAI · Anthropic · Google · AWS Bedrock · Azure OpenAI). Covers data-handling (no training on customer data), model-version pinning, deprecation-notice SLAs, incident-disclosure obligations, audit-rights, attestation cadence, and SOC 2 / ISO 42001 evidence requirements.

### Tests

Seven new per-pattern tests (`test_lda_search.py`, `test_llm_disparate_impact_harness.py`, `test_effective_challenge_harness.py`, `test_vendor_attestation_ledger.py`, `test_retraining_cadence_monitor.py`, `test_deprecation_watch.py`, `test_customer_facing_chatbot_guardrail.py`) lift into the existing 90% coverage gate.

### Voice + brand discipline

- Banned-term lint surface unchanged; the new modules + docs pass `scripts/banned_term_lint.py` clean.
- Tamper-language lint surface unchanged; the v1.1 "tamper-detecting hash-chain (within-trust-boundary)" hedging discipline continues to hold across the new v1.3 prose.

## Upgrade notes

### Backward compatibility

v1.3 is backward-compatible with v1.2. No breaking API changes. All v1.2 imports continue to resolve. The new `AuditEventType.DEPRECATION_ALERT` enum value is additive; existing consumers that match exhaustively on `AuditEventType` will need a one-line update.

### No new runtime dependencies

The framework's default install remains zero-runtime-dependency. The optional extras (`[mcp]`, `[otel]`, `[all-integrations]`) are unchanged from v1.2.

### CLI tool

`finserv-audit verify` and `finserv-audit maturity` are unchanged from v1.2.

### CI impact

Adopters mirroring the CI workflow get seven additional per-pattern tests inside the existing `pytest` step; no separate job. Coverage gate remains 90%.

## Manual follow-ups for the maintainer

1. **PyPI publish via Trusted Publishing** — same flow as v1.2 (`.github/workflows/publish.yml`). Two-stage: TestPyPI smoke -> required-reviewer gate -> production PyPI.
2. **Zenodo DOI re-issue** — link the new tag to the existing Zenodo record so the DOI badge resolves to v1.3.0.
3. **Refresh this release-notes body** — replace the anticipated bullets with the actual shipped surface once Tranches A-D commits land. Anything not shipped in this tranche moves to the "deferred to v1.4" list.
4. **README badges** — Sigstore-signed + PyPI version badges remain in place from v1.2; no new badge work required.

## What's deferred to v1.4 — Operational Refinements

- **`ProtectedClassProxyDetector` SHAP / CDD arms** — per ADR-0019 v1.2 reconciliation deferral; v1.3 ships the LDA arm via `LDASearchHarness`, SHAP + CDD remain deferred.
- **LDA-search continuous-feature quantile-binning helper** — per `ProtectedClassProxyDetector` v1.2 docstring deferral.
- **NAIC Model Bulletin on Use of AI Systems by Insurers** — insurance-vertical mapping (carried from prior v2.0 plan; promoted to v1.4 because the regulatory pressure has materialized).
- **PCAOB AS 2201 amendment overlay** — separate `ASSURANCE-GUIDE` appendix once the final amendment text is published.
- **Additional state-AG enforcement cases** as they emerge.

## What's deferred to v2.0 — Agentic-AI Ecosystem + Platform Surfaces

- **Agentic-AI ecosystem adapters** — Google A2A · LangGraph · Microsoft Agent Framework (MAF) · CrewAI
- **AIBOM generator** — AI Bill of Materials per the emerging CISA + EU specs
- **FastAPI governance endpoint** — REST surface exposing DEFCON state, veto log, audit-chain verification
- **Kubernetes operator** — DEFCON as a Kubernetes custom resource with controller-driven escalation
- **Adversarial test pack** — red-team scenarios per ADR-0018 threat model
- **PE portfolio playbook** — operating-partner-facing rollout sequence for portco AI governance
